prs.title: Fix: Add try/catch around stat() in Windows symlink logic

labels.name: fs
labels.description: Issues and PRs related to the fs subsystem / file system.

labels.name: needs-ci
labels.description: PRs that need a full CI run.

prs.body:
### 🛠 Fix: Add defensive try/catch around `stat()` in Windows symlink logic

This PR adds a small defensive wrapper around the `stat()` call used during
symlink creation on Windows. In certain environments, `stat()` may throw
synchronously rather than returning an error, which can crash the Node.js
process. Catching this ensures the code fails safely instead of terminating.

#### Why this matters
- **Prevents rare but real crashes:** handles synchronous `stat()` throws.
- **Graceful fallback:** falls back to the standard symlink creation path.
- **Minimal, targeted change:** only adds a simple try/catch and comment.
- **Windows-specific:** no impact on other platforms or code paths.

#### Summary
A small but meaningful defensive improvement that improves Windows stability
while preserving existing behavior and keeping the change surface minimal.

prs.title: fix: increase timeout in flaky aggregation cursor test (gh-8835)
prs.body:
This PR fixes the flaky test "closing aggregation cursor emits close event only once (gh-8835)".

### Problem
The test in `test/query.cursor.test.js` used a 20ms timeout to wait for the `close` event.
In slower CI environments, the event was not always emitted within this short window, causing:
  
  AssertionError: 0 == 1

This made the test unreliable and caused intermittent CI failures.

### Fix
Increased the timeout from 20ms to 200ms, matching other similar cursor-related tests in the same file.
setTimeout(() => {
  assert.equal(closeEventTriggeredCount, 1);
  done();
}, 200);
This provides sufficient time for the event to fire in CI environments.

### Result
This change stabilizes the test and prevents false negatives during CI runs.

### Branch
`fix-flaky-aggregation-cursor-test`

Commit: `215c7fa29`

prs.body:
## Fixes ENOENT errors when using fs.cp(), fs.cpSync(), and fs.promises.cp() on ASAR paths

This PR adds ASAR support for the newer Node.js copy APIs (`fs.cp`, `fs.cpSync`, and `fs.promises.cp`).  
These methods were not previously wrapped by Electron’s ASAR filesystem layer, which caused copy operations targeting paths inside `app.asar` to fail with `ENOENT`.  
The change brings these APIs in line with the existing ASAR-aware wrappers such as `readFile`, `copyFile`, and `readdir`.

---

## Description of Change

Fixes #49012

Electron’s ASAR implementation overrides a subset of Node’s `fs` APIs so that operations on paths inside `.asar` archives resolve correctly.  
However, the newer `fs.cp` family of methods was not included in this wrapper, resulting in unhandled real-filesystem lookups when attempting to copy files or directories from within an ASAR archive.

This PR implements ASAR-aware versions of:

- `fs.cp`
- `fs.cpSync`
- `fs.promises.cp`

and adds tests to ensure these methods behave consistently with the rest of the ASAR virtual filesystem.

---

## Checklist

- [x] PR description included  
- [x] `npm test` passes  
- [x] Tests are added or updated where appropriate  
- [ ] Documentation updates (not required unless a public API changes)  
- [x] Release notes added following Electron’s style guide  

---

## Release Notes

Notes: Added ASAR support for `fs.cp()`, `fs.cpSync()`, and `fs.promises.cp()`.

prs.title: fix: cap onUploadProgress at 100% when loaded exceeds total
prs.body:
##  Overview of the Changes

- Corrects the progress calculation in `progressEventReducer` to ensure the computed percentage never exceeds 100%.
- Adds a `Math.min()` clamp so the normalized progress value is always capped at `1.0`.
- Introduces a regression test to cover the overflow scenario and prevent similar issues in future updates.
- Handles a React Native 0.81.4 edge case where `loaded` bytes can temporarily exceed `total`, resulting in inflated progress values.

---

##  Why This Matters

In some environments — most notably React Native 0.81.4 — progress events can report `loaded > total` due to momentary inconsistencies in native upload tracking.  
Without a safety check, Axios ends up emitting progress values like:

prs.title: clear localStorage on login
prs.body:
PR Comment for #178:
Tested and confirmed that localStorage is now correctly cleared upon each login.
Switched to using a fixed key strategy for localStorage to avoid UID issues.
This ensures user data from previous sessions doesn’t persist across logins.
Also verified that the profile form resets correctly when logging in with a different account.

prs.title: Responsive fixes implemented
prs.body:
PR #173: Responsive Fixes Implemented
Improved responsiveness for the search bar and container layout, especially on iPad Pro and other tablet screens.
Ensured that black/dark sections remain constrained without expanding the outer white container.
Refined styles for mobile and tablet breakpoints using scoped component CSS.

prs.title: Bio Generator Integration & UI Fixes
prs.body:
PR #146: Bio Generator Integration & UI Fixes
Integrated frontend with backend /generate API for short, professional bios.
Refined prompt to limit output to ~50 words for LinkedIn/resume use.
Fixed minor UI issues (textarea count, loading state, result display).
Added error handling, copy & regenerate functionality.

prs.title: Fix careerboost search
prs.body:
*Fixed careerboost page Issue #115
Fixed the issue where the suggestions dropdown didn't disappear after selection or pressing Enter
Ensured the dropdown stays positioned above the search bar as intended
Handled cases where typing beyond the match still showed stale suggestions
Improved overall dropdown behavior and user interaction for role search

prs.title: Fix login refresh issue
prs.body:
PR #111 – Fix: Persistent Login State After Refresh
Implemented onAuthStateChanged from Firebase to track authentication status.
Preserved user session across page reloads, improving user experience.
Updated App.jsx to manage isSignedIn state and pass it to Navbar and routes.
This resolves the issue where users were unexpectedly logged out on page refresh.

prs.title: added google signin
prs.body:
PR #104 – Feature: Google Sign-In Integration
Added Google Sign-In using Firebase Authentication and signInWithPopup.
Captured basic user info (name, email, phone) and stored it in Firestore.
Integrated handleGoogleSignup in the Signup component with proper loading and error handling.
Ensured smooth navigation to Home after successful sign-in.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added real-time authentication state monitoring using Firebase, ensuring the app responds to user sign-in and sign-out events.
  * Implemented user login with Firebase email and password authentication, providing error handling and loading indicators.
  * Updated Google sign-up to use Firebase's popup-based authentication for a smoother registration experience, with improved error handling and loading feedback.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved error messages for invalid login credentials.

* **Style**
  * Minor cleanup of debug comments in the Navbar and authentication-related components.
